{{Infobox location
| name = Crimson Vault
| introduced = House of Blades
}}

''See also: [[Crimson Vault Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Crimson [[Vault]], also known as Ragnarus, is a unique [[Territory]] that serves as the exclusive domain of the [[Kings of Damasca]]. Access to this realm is restricted by bloodline, as only those of the royal lineage possess the ability to open its [[Gates]]. Within this world, powerful weapons are stored on marble shelves, though these items often come with significant consequences. For example, a seal established by the first [[King of Damasca]] requires an annual blood sacrifice to maintain security. The physical entrance to the [[Vault]] is characterized by a rough stone cave containing silver double doors. These doors are decorated with the detailed portrait of a long-deceased, one-eyed, bearded king and are flanked by two torches that emit an unnatural crimson light. Once inside, the interior features a long marble hallway or gallery with shelving for weapons and is illuminated by the same scarlet light. [[Travelers]] can only open a Gate to the Crimson [[Vault]] from within the primary world, not from other [[Territories]]. Furthermore, movement through the [[Vault]] is restricted; any traveler entering the Crimson [[Vault]] can only exit into the capital of [[Damasca]], specifically the palace in [[Cana]]. While the realm is a source of formidable weaponry, it has also been associated with destructive forces and gruesome elements, such as the bloody [[Tree]] of Ragnarus.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The [[Crimson Vault]] is another name for Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The Crimson [[Vault]] serves as the origin of the red stone embedded in [[King]] [[Zakareth's eye]] and is illuminated by unnatural scarlet torches. While the prophecy of the Rising Sun foretells the end of its reign, the location is accessed through a specific Gate that can only be opened within the world rather than the [[Territories]]. This site, which resembles a massive cave featuring silver doors and two red-burning torches, contains various fragments. Although [[Leah]]'s appointment as [[Successor]] was sworn in its presence before she departed from her father, the [[vault]]'s silver doors were eventually discovered cracked open. [[Talos]] later managed to open a Gate to the [[vault]] by utilizing red light.}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] is the source of the red stone used in [[King]] [[Zakareth's eye]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] is lit by unnatural scarlet torches.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The reign of the [[Crimson Vault]] is coming to an end according to the prophecy of the Rising Sun.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] is accessed via the Gate to the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c8">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The appointment of [[Leah]] as [[Successor]] was sworn in the presence of the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]] left the [[Crimson Vault]] and her father.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|A Gate to the [[Crimson Vault]] can only be opened in the world, not in the [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] contains fragments.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Talos]] opened a Gate to the [[Crimson Vault]] using red light.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] resembles a huge cave with two red-burning torches and a silver door.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] has silver doors.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Crimson Vault]] has silver doors that were found cracked open.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The Crimson [[Vault]] is an ancient, dangerous territory consisting of red rock that appears soaked in blood. Forbidden by [[Elysia]] and rarely visited by the [[Eldest]], this location serves as a gateway to Ragnarus and houses silver doors carved with the face of a one-eyed king. Within its halls, rows of weapons rest on marble shelves, and it serves as a place where blades can be sealed or where the [[Lightning Spear]] was once kept. While [[Zakareth]] departed the [[vault]] to find his palace in ruins, [[Indirial]] was pulled inside by a band of ice, only to have his strength scattered by the [[vault]]'s power. The site remains a place of mystery and potential enemies, featuring scarlet light chains that burst from its depths and a connection to a red stone mentioned by the [[Eldest]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is a place the [[Eldest]] has not visited recently.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c1">''City of Light'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] left the [[Crimson Vault]] to find his palace in ruins.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The door to the [[Crimson Vault]] leads to Ragnarus.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3">''City of Light'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The power of the [[Crimson Vault]] is weaker in a foreign [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is an old [[Territory]] that was forbidden by [[Elysia]] because it was too dangerous.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10">''City of Light'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is a location where the [[Lightning Spear]] was kept.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] may contain enemies.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c12">''City of Light'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Chains of scarlet light burst out of the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] contains silver doors carved with the face of a one-eyed king.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was pulled into the [[Crimson Vault]] by a band of ice.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|There is much in the [[Crimson Vault]] that [[Indirial]] is not aware of.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Power from the [[Crimson Vault]] scattered the strength of [[Indirial]]'s kick.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is a place [[Kai]] intends to tear apart.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is a stretch of rock dyed red as though soaked in blood.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] walks through the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] contains rows of weapons on marble shelves.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Crimson Vault]] is a location where blades can be sealed.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The [[Eldest]] mentioned the [[Crimson Vault]] in connection to a red stone.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“The prophecy of the Rising Sun has already borne fruit. The reign of the Crimson Vault has almost come to an end.”<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“A fragment of the Crimson Vault?” the Eldest asked, sounding surprised.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|"Ragnarus," she said. "That was the door to the Crimson Vault."<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|"A place that we of Elysia had forbidden years before, on the grounds that it was too dangerous. The Crimson Vault."<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c10" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|"It seems they were sealed inside the Crimson Vault."<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate locations]]
